critic.verdict: A mostly standard Easy chart that is severely held back by excessive repetition and uncomfortable, high-difficulty spikes including Up-Down jumps.

issues.problem: The use of JUMP UD (Up/Down) is highly discouraged for Easy charts, as it requires unsafe/awkward body twisting for beginners.
The sequence 'JUMP LR -> tap D -> JUMP LR -> tap D' is copy-pasted excessively, leading to an extremely repetitive and boring gameplay flow.
Transitioning directly from a JUMP UD into dense quarter-note steps (L -> R -> D) ending in another jump at 176 BPM represents a massive, unfair spike in difficulty for an Easy chart.

critic.verdict: A highly repetitive chart that matches the rhythm of the music but suffers from severe monotony and lazy patterning.

issues.problem: Extreme repetition of the 'JUMP LR -> tap D' sequence. Repeating the exact same physical movement 44 times in a row is incredibly monotonous and exhausting.
Severe lack of jump variety. Almost all jumps are 'JUMP LR', which makes the layout feel one-dimensional and fails to utilize the full pad.
Awkward foot flow. Repeatedly jumping on L+R and immediately tapping D forces the player into repetitive double-stepping or straining bracket-steps.

critic.verdict: The chart is heavily marred by physically impossible three-panel holds (unintentional hands) and extreme, lazy pattern repetition.

issues.problem: Requires pressing Left and Right simultaneously while holding Down (from 9.736), creating an impossible three-panel note for pad play without using hands.
Requires tapping Down while both Up (15.444) and Right (15.700) are being held, forcing an awkward three-panel press.
Extremely repetitive looping of the exact same 'L -> R -> U -> D' pattern for over 10 seconds, which feels lazy and monotonous.
Another lengthy, repetitive 'U -> D -> L -> R' pattern loop lasting over 10 seconds during a major section of the song.
Holding Down while performing rapid taps on Up, Left, and Right forces highly awkward, unbalanced crossovers.
